101.plain: Stock arriving against a purchase order or production order — the standard "goods have turned up" posting.
101.sap: Goods receipt for purchase order or order

103.plain: Receiving goods against a purchase order into a blocked holding area for inspection before they're released to normal stock; not usable for stock transport orders.
103.sap: Goods receipt for purchase order into GR blocked stock

105.plain: Releasing goods that were sitting in GR blocked stock into normal usable stock, once they've been approved — it behaves just like a standard goods receipt.
105.sap: Release from GR blocked stock for purchase order

121.plain: A follow-up correction posting for subcontracting quantities, generated automatically by the system rather than entered by a user.

122.plain: Sending goods back to the supplier or back to production against a purchase order or production order, kept separate from a straight cancellation of the original receipt.
122.sap: Return delivery to supplier or to production

123.plain: Undoing a previously posted return delivery, putting the stock quantities back the way they were before the return was recorded.

124.plain: Sending goods that were sitting in GR blocked stock back to the vendor, reversing what had been received into that blocked status.
124.sap: Return delivery to vendor from GR blocked stock

125.plain: Reversing a return delivery that was previously posted from GR blocked stock back to the vendor, restoring the blocked stock.
125.sap: Return delivery from GR blocked stock - reversal

131.plain: An automatic goods receipt posting tied to a run schedule header — created by the system when that receipt is confirmed, not something a user enters directly.
131.sap: Goods receipt for run schedule header

141.plain: An automatic correction the system posts for an active ingredient when it detects that a goods receipt caused too much or too little component consumption.
141.sap: Goods receipt for subsequent adjustment for active ingredient

161.plain: Sending goods back to the vendor against a purchase order line that was flagged as a returns item, posted at the same time as the original receipt.

201.plain: Stock being pulled out of the warehouse and charged to a cost center, drawn only from stock that is free and available for use.
201.sap: Goods issue for a cost center

221.plain: Stock being issued and charged to a project, taken only from unrestricted, freely usable inventory rather than blocked or inspection stock.

231.plain: Stock leaving the warehouse for a customer order when the delivery isn't being handled through the Shipping component.
231.sap: Goods issue for a customer order (without Shipping)

241.plain: Stock being issued and charged against a fixed asset record, drawn only from stock that is free and available for use.

251.plain: Stock sold and issued without a formal sales order in the system, normally posted against a cost center by default.
251.sap: Goods issue for sales (without customer order)

261.plain: Components being consumed by a production order — stock leaves the warehouse and goes into the order.

291.plain: A flexible goods issue where every account-assignment field is open, letting you charge the stock to whichever cost object you choose.
291.sap: Goods issue for any arbitrary account assignment

301.plain: Moving stock directly from one plant's freely usable inventory straight into another plant's freely usable inventory in a single posting.
301.sap: Transfer posting plant to plant in one step

303.plain: The first half of a two-step plant transfer: stock leaves the sending plant's usable inventory and sits in transit until it's received.
303.sap: Transfer posting plant to plant in two steps - removal from storage

305.plain: The second half of a two-step plant transfer: stock in transit is received into the new plant's usable stock, with no additional valuation posted.
305.sap: Transfer posting plant to plant in two steps - placement in storage

309.plain: Reclassifying stock from one material number to another, moving the quantity out of one material's usable stock and into another's.

311.plain: Moving stock directly between two storage locations within the same plant, from one location's usable stock straight into the other's, in one posting.
311.sap: Transfer posting storage location to storage location in one step

313.plain: The first step of a two-step storage location transfer: stock leaves the source location's usable inventory and is held as stock in transfer.
313.sap: Stock transfer storage location to storage location in two steps - removal from storage

315.plain: The second step of a two-step storage location transfer: stock that was in transit is placed into the receiving location's usable inventory.
315.sap: Transfer posting storage location to storage location in two steps - placement in storage

317.plain: In retail, combining separate component materials back into a single structured product, reversing a previous split into components.
317.sap: Creation of a structured material from constituent components (Retail)

319.plain: In retail, breaking a structured product apart into its individual component materials, entered manually when needed.

321.plain: Stock that passed quality inspection being released into freely usable inventory, optionally moving it to a different storage location at the same time.
321.sap: Transfer posting stock in quality inspection - unrestricted-use stock

323.plain: Moving stock that is still under quality inspection from one storage location to another, without changing its inspection status.
323.sap: Transfer posting storage location to storage location - stock in quality inspection

325.plain: Moving blocked stock from one storage location to another while it stays blocked and unavailable for use.
325.sap: Transfer posting storage location to storage location - blocked stock

331.plain: Pulling a physical sample out of stock that is under quality inspection for testing purposes; because the sample is destroyed, it's treated the same as scrapping.
331.sap: Withdrawal of sample from stock in quality inspection

333.plain: Pulling a physical sample out of freely usable stock for testing; since the sample is destroyed in testing, it's accounted for the same as scrap.
333.sap: Withdrawal of sample from unrestricted-use stock

335.plain: Pulling a physical sample out of blocked stock for testing; because the sample gets destroyed, it's posted the same way as scrapping.
335.sap: Withdrawal of sample from blocked stock

340.plain: Changing which valuation type a batch is assigned to, which the system posts when you edit the batch's data — and this one can't be reversed.

341.plain: An automatic posting that fires when a batch's status flips from available to unavailable, moving its stock from usable into restricted stock.
341.sap: Change in status of a batch (available to unavailable)

343.plain: Releasing blocked stock into freely usable inventory, with the option to move it to a different storage location at the same time.
343.sap: Transfer posting blocked stock - unrestricted-use stock

349.plain: Moving blocked stock into quality inspection status instead of releasing it straight to usable stock, optionally shifting it to another storage location too.
349.sap: Transfer posting from blocked stock to stock in quality inspection

351.plain: Stock leaving one plant's usable inventory for a stock transport order, sitting as in-transit stock until the receiving plant takes it in, without using Shipping.
351.sap: Goods issue for a stock transport order (without Shipping)

411.plain: Converting sales-order stock, vendor consignment stock, or project stock into the company's own regular storage location stock, requiring the matching special stock indicator.
411.sap: Transfer posting of special stocks E, K, and Q to company's own stock

413.plain: Moving stock — whether your own regular inventory, another sales order's stock, consignment stock, or project stock — into a specific sales order's reserved stock.
413.sap: Transfer posting to sales order stock

415.plain: Moving stock — from your own regular inventory, consignment stock, or another project's stock — into a specific project's reserved stock.

441.plain: Reclassifying returnable empties, such as bottles or containers, as tied to a specific filled product rather than tracked as general loose empties stock.
441.sap: Transfer posting non-tied to tied empties

451.plain: Recording goods a customer sent back when the return isn't processed through the Shipping component, landing the stock in blocked returns status.

453.plain: Releasing customer-returned stock out of blocked-returns status into normal usable, valuated inventory once it's been checked and accepted.
453.sap: Transfer posting blocked stock returns to unrestricted-use stock

455.plain: Moving customer-returned stock that is still in blocked-returns status from one storage location to another, without changing its blocked status.
455.sap: Transfer posting storage location to storage location - blocked stock returns

457.plain: Sending customer-returned stock out of blocked-returns status into quality inspection, converting it to valuated stock in the process.
457.sap: Transfer posting blocked stock returns to quality inspection stock

459.plain: Converting customer-returned stock from blocked-returns status into standard blocked stock, which brings the quantity into the company's valuated inventory records.
459.sap: Transfer posting blocked stock returns to blocked stock

501.plain: Receiving stock straight into usable inventory from a vendor delivery that has no purchase order behind it, so the invoice can't be matched against it later.
501.sap: Goods receipt without purchase order - unrestricted-use stock

503.plain: Receiving stock into quality inspection from a vendor delivery with no purchase order behind it, meaning the invoice can't later be matched to this receipt.
503.sap: Goods receipt without purchase order - stock in quality inspection

505.plain: Receiving stock into blocked status from a vendor delivery that has no purchase order behind it, so it can't be matched to an invoice later.
505.sap: Goods receipt without purchase order - blocked stock

511.plain: Stock received from a vendor at no cost, added to usable inventory — if the material uses moving average pricing, that average price drops as a result.

521.plain: Stock coming out of production straight into usable inventory when there's no production order tied to the receipt.
521.sap: Goods receipt without order - unrestricted-use stock

523.plain: Stock coming out of production and placed into quality inspection when there is no production order behind the delivery.
523.sap: Goods receipt without order - stock in quality inspection

525.plain: Stock coming out of production and placed into blocked status when there is no production order behind the delivery.
525.sap: Goods receipt without order - blocked stock

531.plain: Recording a by-product generated during production, normally linked back to the production order though it can also be posted with no order reference.
531.sap: Goods receipt from by-product from the order

541.plain: Sending component materials from your own usable stock out to a subcontractor, so they have what they need to complete a subcontract order.
541.sap: Transfer posting unrestricted-use stock - stock of material provided to vendor

543.plain: An automatic posting that records components previously sent to a subcontractor as consumed, rather than something a user enters directly.
543.sap: Consumption from stock of material provided to vendor

545.plain: Recording a by-product returned from a subcontractor into the stock provided to that vendor, triggered when a component was entered with a negative quantity on the subcontract order.
545.sap: Goods receipt from by-product from subcontracting

551.plain: Writing off good stock as scrap; the quantity leaves inventory and the cost is posted to a scrap account.

553.plain: Writing off stock that was sitting in quality inspection as scrap; the quantity leaves inventory and the cost posts to a scrap account.
553.sap: Scrapping from stock in quality inspection

555.plain: Writing off blocked stock as scrap; the quantity leaves inventory and the cost posts to a scrap account, the same as scrapping any other stock.

561.plain: Loading an opening stock quantity into usable inventory for the first time, such as when a material is first set up in the system.
561.sap: Initial entry of stock - unrestricted-use stock

563.plain: Loading an opening stock quantity directly into quality inspection status when first setting up a material's stock in the system.
563.sap: Initial entry of stock - quality inspection

565.plain: Loading an opening stock quantity directly into blocked status when first setting up a material's stock in the system.
565.sap: Initial entry of stock - blocked stock

571.plain: Stock completed against an assembly order landing in usable inventory, posted only through the repetitive manufacturing goods-receipt-for-sales-order function, not directly in Inventory Management.
571.sap: Goods receipt for assembly order to unrestricted-use

573.plain: Stock completed against an assembly order landing in quality inspection, posted only through the repetitive manufacturing goods-receipt-for-sales-order function, not directly in Inventory Management.
573.sap: Goods receipt for assembly order to quality inspection

575.plain: Stock completed against an assembly order landing in blocked status, posted only through the repetitive manufacturing goods-receipt-for-sales-order function, not directly in Inventory Management.
575.sap: Goods receipt for assembly order to blocked stock

581.plain: Recording a by-product coming out of a project network, normally linked back to that network though it can also be posted without a reference.
581.sap: Goods receipt of a by-product from the network

601.plain: Stock leaving the warehouse as part of a customer delivery, posted automatically by the Shipping component when the delivery is confirmed.

621.plain: Sending returnable packaging like pallets or crates out to a customer, moving it from your own usable stock into packaging stock held at the customer.

623.plain: Recording returnable packaging as consumed or used up at the customer's site, reducing the returnable packaging stock tracked there.
623.sap: Goods issue from returnable packaging stock at customer (Shipping)

631.plain: Sending stock out to a customer as consignment inventory, moving it from your own usable stock into stock held at the customer's location.
631.sap: Transfer posting unrestricted use - customer consignment stock (Shipping)

633.plain: Recording consignment stock at the customer as consumed, reducing the usable consignment stock that had been held at their site.

641.plain: Stock leaving one plant's usable inventory for a stock transport order processed through Shipping, sitting as in-transit stock until the receiving plant takes it in.
641.sap: Goods issue for a stock transport order (Shipping)

651.plain: Recording goods a customer sent back through a Shipping returns delivery, landing the stock in blocked-returns status.

653.plain: Recording a customer return processed through Shipping and posting it straight into usable, valuated stock rather than a blocked holding status.
653.sap: Returns from customer (Shipping) to unrestricted-use stock

655.plain: Recording a customer return processed through Shipping and posting it directly into quality inspection as valuated stock, rather than a blocked holding status.
655.sap: Returns from customer (Shipping) to stock in quality inspection

657.plain: Recording a customer return processed through Shipping and posting it directly into blocked stock, but still as valuated inventory rather than blocked-returns status.
657.sap: Returns from customer (Shipping) to blocked stock

661.plain: Sending goods back to a vendor with no reference to the original purchase order, where the return is processed as a proper delivery through Shipping.

671.plain: Handling a returns item within a stock transport order that's processed via Shipping, moving the returned quantity back as stock in transit to the issuing plant.
671.sap: Returns for stock transport order via Shipping

677.plain: Completing, on the issuing plant's side, a one-step stock transport order return — the returned quantity lands back in that plant's usable stock.
677.sap: Returns for stock transport order in one step (Shipping)

701.plain: An automatic adjustment posted when a physical inventory count finds a discrepancy in usable stock, correcting the system quantity to match what was counted.

703.plain: An automatic adjustment posted when a physical inventory count finds a discrepancy in stock that's under quality inspection, correcting the system quantity.
703.sap: Inventory differences in quality inspection stock (MM-IM)

707.plain: An automatic adjustment posted when a physical inventory count finds a discrepancy in blocked stock, correcting the system quantity to match what was counted.

711.plain: An automatic adjustment to usable stock quantities, posted when a warehouse-management inventory count uncovers a discrepancy against the system record.

713.plain: An automatic adjustment to quality-inspection stock quantities, posted when a warehouse-management inventory count uncovers a discrepancy against the system record.
713.sap: Inventory differences in quality inspection stock (LE-WM)

715.plain: An automatic adjustment to blocked-returns stock quantities, posted when a warehouse-management inventory count uncovers a discrepancy against the system record.
715.sap: Inventory differences in blocked stock returns (LE-WM)

717.plain: An automatic adjustment to blocked stock quantities, posted when a warehouse-management inventory count uncovers a discrepancy against the system record.

721.plain: In retail, an automatic posting that revalues a value-only material's total sales price on a given date, without affecting its margin.
721.sap: - Sales value receipt - not affecting margins (Retail)

731.plain: In retail, an automatic posting that partially revalues a value-only material's sales price on a given date, this time affecting its margin.
